title: malva alcea subsp. moschata
intro: Malva alcea subsp. moschata, also known as Musk Mallow, is a subspecies of the Common Mallow plant, characterized by its fragrant, musky scent and soft, pale pink flowers. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, soothing, and protective properties, particularly in treating skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. Malva alcea subsp. moschata is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, calming digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or salve, and is also used in herbal teas and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
